001/*
002 * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more
003 * contributor license agreements.  See the NOTICE file distributed with
004 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.
005 * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0
006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with
007 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
008 *
009 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
010 *
011 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
013 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
014 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.commons.reflect;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.function.Suppliers.*;
020import static org.apache.juneau.commons.reflect.ClassArrayFormat.*;
021import static org.apache.juneau.commons.reflect.ClassNameFormat.*;
022import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.AssertionUtils.*;
023import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.CollectionUtils.*;
024import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.Shorts.*;
025import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.ThrowableUtils.*;
026
027import java.lang.annotation.*;
028import java.lang.reflect.*;
029import java.util.*;
030import java.util.function.*;
031import java.util.stream.*;
032
033import org.apache.juneau.commons.function.*;
034import org.apache.juneau.commons.inject.*;
035import org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.*;
036
037/**
038 * Lightweight utility class for introspecting information about a Java field.
039 *
040 * <p>
041 * This class provides a convenient wrapper around {@link Field} that extends the standard Java reflection
042 * API with additional functionality for field introspection, annotation handling, and value access.
043 * It extends {@link AccessibleInfo} to provide {@link AccessibleObject} functionality for accessing
044 * private fields.
045 *
046 * <h5 class='section'>Features:</h5>
047 * <ul class='spaced-list'>
048 *    <li>Field introspection - access field metadata, type, modifiers
049 *    <li>Annotation support - get annotations declared on the field
050 *    <li>Value access - get and set field values with type safety
051 *    <li>Accessibility control - make private fields accessible
052 *    <li>Thread-safe - instances are immutable and safe for concurrent access
053 * </ul>
054 *
055 * <h5 class='section'>Use Cases:</h5>
056 * <ul class='spaced-list'>
057 *    <li>Introspecting field metadata for code generation or analysis
058 *    <li>Accessing field values in beans or data objects
059 *    <li>Finding annotations on fields
060 *    <li>Working with field types and modifiers
061 *    <li>Building frameworks that need to analyze or manipulate field values
062 * </ul>
063 *
064 * <h5 class='section'>Usage:</h5>
065 * <p class='bjava'>
066 *    <jc>// Get FieldInfo from a class</jc>
067 *    ClassInfo <jv>ci</jv> = ClassInfo.<jsm>of</jsm>(MyClass.<jk>class</jk>);
068 *    FieldInfo <jv>field</jv> = <jv>ci</jv>.getField(<js>"myField"</js>);
069 *
070 *    <jc>// Get field type</jc>
071 *    ClassInfo <jv>type</jv> = <jv>field</jv>.getType();
072 *
073 *    <jc>// Get annotations</jc>
074 *    List&lt;AnnotationInfo&lt;MyAnnotation&gt;&gt; <jv>annotations</jv> =
075 *       <jv>field</jv>.getAnnotations(MyAnnotation.<jk>class</jk>).toList();
076 *
077 *    <jc>// Access field value</jc>
078 *    MyClass <jv>obj</jv> = <jk>new</jk> MyClass();
079 *    <jv>field</jv>.accessible();  <jc>// Make accessible if private</jc>
080 *    Object <jv>value</jv> = <jv>field</jv>.get(<jv>obj</jv>);
081 * </p>
082 *
083 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
084 *    <li class='jc'>{@link ClassInfo} - Class introspection
085 *    <li class='jc'>{@link MethodInfo} - Method introspection
086 *    <li class='jc'>{@link ConstructorInfo} - Constructor introspection
087 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/JuneauCommonsReflection">Reflection Package</a>
088 * </ul>
089 */
090@SuppressWarnings({
091   "java:S115",  // Constants use UPPER_snakeCase convention (e.g., CONST_value)
092   "java:S3011"  // Reflection access needed for field introspection
093})
094public final class FieldInfo extends AccessibleInfo implements Comparable<FieldInfo>, Annotatable {
095
096   // Argument name constants for assertArgNotNull
097   private static final String ARG_declaringClass = "declaringClass";
098   private static final String ARG_inner = "inner";
099
100   /**
101    * Creates a FieldInfo wrapper for the specified field.
102    *
103    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
104    * <p class='bjava'>
105    *    ClassInfo <jv>ci</jv> = ClassInfo.<jsm>of</jsm>(MyClass.<jk>class</jk>);
106    *    Field <jv>f</jv> = MyClass.<jk>class</jk>.getField(<js>"myField"</js>);
107    *    FieldInfo <jv>fi</jv> = FieldInfo.<jsm>of</jsm>(<jv>ci</jv>, <jv>f</jv>);
108    * </p>
109    *
110    * @param declaringClass The ClassInfo for the class that declares this field. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
111    * @param inner The field being wrapped. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
112    * @return A new FieldInfo object wrapping the field.
113    */
114   public static FieldInfo of(ClassInfo declaringClass, Field inner) {
115      assertArgNotNull(ARG_declaringClass, declaringClass);
116      return declaringClass.getField(inner);
117   }
118
119   /**
120    * Creates a FieldInfo wrapper for the specified field.
121    *
122    * <p>
123    * This convenience method automatically determines the declaring class from the field.
124    *
125    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
126    * <p class='bjava'>
127    *    Field <jv>f</jv> = MyClass.<jk>class</jk>.getField(<js>"myField"</js>);
128    *    FieldInfo <jv>fi</jv> = FieldInfo.<jsm>of</jsm>(<jv>f</jv>);
129    * </p>
130    *
131    * @param inner The field being wrapped. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
132    * @return A new FieldInfo object wrapping the field.
133    */
134   public static FieldInfo of(Field inner) {
135      assertArgNotNull(ARG_inner, inner);
136      return ClassInfo.of(inner.getDeclaringClass()).getField(inner);
137   }
138
139   private final Field inner;
140   private final ClassInfo declaringClass;
141   private final Supplier<ClassInfo> type;
142   private final Supplier<List<AnnotationInfo<Annotation>>> annotations;  // All annotations declared directly on this field.
143   private final Supplier<String> nameFull;  // Fully qualified field name (declaring-class.field-name).
144   private final Supplier<String> toString;  // String representation with modifiers, type, and full name.
145
146   /**
147    * Constructor.
148    *
149    * <p>
150    * Creates a new FieldInfo wrapper for the specified field. This constructor is protected
151    * and should not be called directly. Use the static factory methods {@link #of(Field)} or
152    * obtain FieldInfo instances from {@link ClassInfo#getField(Field)}.
153    *
154    * @param declaringClass The ClassInfo for the class that declares this field.
155    * @param inner The field being wrapped.
156    */
157   protected FieldInfo(ClassInfo declaringClass, Field inner) {
158      super(inner, inner.getModifiers());
159      assertArgNotNull(ARG_inner, inner);
160      this.declaringClass = declaringClass;
161      this.inner = inner;
162      this.type = memoize(() -> ClassInfo.of(inner.getType(), inner.getGenericType()));
163      this.annotations = memoize(() -> stream(inner.getAnnotations()).flatMap(AnnotationUtils::streamRepeated).map(a -> ai(this, a)).toList());
164      this.nameFull = memoize(this::findNameFull);
165      this.toString = memoize(this::findToString);
166   }
167
168   /**
169    * Attempts to call <code>x.setAccessible(<jk>true</jk>)</code> and quietly ignores security exceptions.
170    *
171    * @return This object.
172    */
173   public FieldInfo accessible() {
174      setAccessible();
175      return this;
176   }
177
178   @Override
179   public int compareTo(FieldInfo o) {
180      return cmp(getName(), o.getName());
181   }
182
183   /**
184    * Returns the field value on the specified object.
185    *
186    * <p>
187    * If the underlying field is static, then the specified <c>o</c> argument is ignored. It may be <jk>null</jk>.
188    *
189    * @param <T> The object type to retrieve.
190    * @param o The object containing the field.  Can be <jk>null</jk> for static fields.
191    * @return The field value.
192    * @throws BeanRuntimeException Field was not accessible or field does not belong to object.
193    */
194   @SuppressWarnings({
195      "unchecked" // Type erasure requires cast to T for field retrieval
196   })
197   public <T> T get(Object o) throws BeanRuntimeException {
198      return safe(() -> {
199         inner.setAccessible(true);
200         return (T)inner.get(o);
201      }, e -> brex(e));
202   }
203
204   @Override /* Annotatable */
205   public AnnotatableType getAnnotatableType() { return AnnotatableType.FIELD_TYPE; }
206
207   /**
208    * Returns an {@link AnnotatedType} object that represents the use of a type to specify the declared type of the field.
209    *
210    * <p>
211    * Same as calling {@link Field#getAnnotatedType()}.
212    *
213    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
214    * <p class='bjava'>
215    *    <jc>// Get annotated type: @NotNull String name</jc>
216    *    FieldInfo <jv>fi</jv> = ClassInfo.<jsm>of</jsm>(MyClass.<jk>class</jk>).getField(<js>"name"</js>);
217    *    AnnotatedType <jv>aType</jv> = <jv>fi</jv>.getAnnotatedType();
218    *    <jc>// Check for @NotNull on the type</jc>
219    * </p>
220    *
221    * @return An {@link AnnotatedType} object representing the declared type.
222    * @see Field#getAnnotatedType()
223    */
224   public AnnotatedType getAnnotatedType() { return inner.getAnnotatedType(); }
225
226   /**
227    * Returns all annotations declared on this field.
228    *
229    * <p>
230    * <b>Note on Repeatable Annotations:</b>
231    * Repeatable annotations (those marked with {@link Repeatable @Repeatable}) are automatically
232    * expanded into their individual annotation instances. For example, if a field has multiple {@code @Marshalled} annotations,
233    * this method returns each {@code @Marshalled} annotation separately, rather than the container annotation.
234    *
235    * @return
236    *    An unmodifiable list of all annotations declared on this field.
237    *    <br>Repeatable annotations are expanded into individual instances.
238    */
239   public List<AnnotationInfo<Annotation>> getAnnotations() { return annotations.get(); }
240
241   /**
242    * Returns all annotations of the specified type declared on this field.
243    *
244    * @param <A> The annotation type.
245    * @param type The annotation type.
246    * @return A stream of all matching annotations.
247    */
248   @SuppressWarnings({
249      "unchecked" // Type erasure requires cast for annotation stream
250   })
251   public <A extends Annotation> Stream<AnnotationInfo<A>> getAnnotations(Class<A> type) {
252      return annotations.get().stream().filter(x -> type.isInstance(x.inner())).map(x -> (AnnotationInfo<A>)x);
253   }
254
255   /**
256    * Returns metadata about the declaring class.
257    *
258    * @return Metadata about the declaring class.
259    */
260   public ClassInfo getDeclaringClass() { return declaringClass; }
261
262   /**
263    * Returns the type of this field.
264    *
265    * @return The type of this field.
266    */
267   public ClassInfo getFieldType() { return type.get(); }
268
269   /**
270    * Returns the full name of this field.
271    *
272    * <h5 class='section'>Examples:</h5>
273    * <ul>
274    *    <li><js>"com.foo.MyClass.myField"</js> - Method.
275    * </ul>
276    *
277    * @return The underlying executable name.
278    */
279   public String getNameFull() { return nameFull.get(); }
280
281   @Override /* Annotatable */
282   public String getLabel() { return getDeclaringClass().getNameSimple() + "." + getName(); }
283
284   /**
285    * Returns the name of this field.
286    *
287    * @return The name of this field.
288    */
289   public String getName() { return inner.getName(); }
290
291   /**
292    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if the specified annotation is present.
293    *
294    * @param <A> The annotation type to look for.
295    * @param type The annotation to look for.
296    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the specified annotation is present.
297    */
298   public <A extends Annotation> boolean hasAnnotation(Class<A> type) {
299      return getAnnotations(type).findAny().isPresent();
300   }
301
302   /**
303    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if the field has the specified name.
304    *
305    * @param name The name to compare against.
306    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the field has the specified name.
307    */
308   public boolean hasName(String name) {
309      return inner.getName().equals(name);
310   }
311
312   /**
313    * Returns the wrapped field.
314    *
315    * @return The wrapped field.
316    */
317   public Field inner() {
318      return inner;
319   }
320
321   /**
322    * Compares this FieldInfo with the specified object for equality.
323    *
324    * <p>
325    * Two FieldInfo objects are considered equal if they wrap the same underlying {@link Field} object.
326    * This delegates to the underlying {@link Field#equals(Object)} method.
327    *
328    * <p>
329    * This method makes FieldInfo suitable for use as keys in hash-based collections such as {@link HashMap}
330    * and {@link HashSet}.
331    *
332    * @param obj The object to compare with.
333    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the objects are equal, <jk>false</jk> otherwise.
334    */
335   @Override
336   public boolean equals(Object obj) {
337      return obj instanceof FieldInfo other && eq(this, other, (x, y) -> eq(x.inner, y.inner));
338   }
339
340   /**
341    * Returns a hash code value for this FieldInfo.
342    *
343    * <p>
344    * This delegates to the underlying {@link Field#hashCode()} method.
345    *
346    * <p>
347    * This method makes FieldInfo suitable for use as keys in hash-based collections such as {@link HashMap}
348    * and {@link HashSet}.
349    *
350    * @return A hash code value for this FieldInfo.
351    */
352   @Override
353   public int hashCode() {
354      return inner.hashCode();
355   }
356
357   /**
358    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if all specified flags are applicable to this field.
359    *
360    * @param flag The flag to test for.
361    * @return <jk>true</jk> if all specified flags are applicable to this field.
362    */
363   @Override
364   public boolean is(ElementFlag flag) {
365      return switch (flag) {
366         case DEPRECATED -> isDeprecated();
367         case NOT_DEPRECATED -> isNotDeprecated();
368         case ENUM_CONSTANT -> isEnumConstant();
369         case NOT_ENUM_CONSTANT -> ! isEnumConstant();
370         case SYNTHETIC -> isSynthetic();
371         case NOT_SYNTHETIC -> ! isSynthetic();  // HTT
372         default -> super.is(flag);
373      };
374   }
375
376   /**
377    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this field has the {@link Deprecated @Deprecated} annotation on it.
378    *
379    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this field has the {@link Deprecated @Deprecated} annotation on it.
380    */
381   public boolean isDeprecated() { return inner.isAnnotationPresent(Deprecated.class); }
382
383   /**
384    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this field represents an element of an enumerated type.
385    *
386    * <p>
387    * Same as calling {@link Field#isEnumConstant()}.
388    *
389    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
390    * <p class='bjava'>
391    *    <jc>// Check if field is an enum constant</jc>
392    *    FieldInfo <jv>fi</jv> = ClassInfo.<jsm>of</jsm>(MyEnum.<jk>class</jk>).getField(<js>"VALUE1"</js>);
393    *    <jk>if</jk> (<jv>fi</jv>.isEnumConstant()) {
394    *       <jc>// Handle enum constant</jc>
395    *    }
396    * </p>
397    *
398    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this field represents an enum constant.
399    * @see Field#isEnumConstant()
400    */
401   public boolean isEnumConstant() { return inner.isEnumConstant(); }
402
403   /**
404    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this field doesn't have the {@link Deprecated @Deprecated} annotation on it.
405    *
406    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this field doesn't have the {@link Deprecated @Deprecated} annotation on it.
407    */
408   public boolean isNotDeprecated() { return ! inner.isAnnotationPresent(Deprecated.class); }
409
410   /**
411    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this field is a synthetic field as defined by the Java Language Specification.
412    *
413    * <p>
414    * Same as calling {@link Field#isSynthetic()}.
415    *
416    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
417    * <p class='bjava'>
418    *    <jc>// Filter out compiler-generated fields</jc>
419    *    FieldInfo <jv>fi</jv> = ...;
420    *    <jk>if</jk> (! <jv>fi</jv>.isSynthetic()) {
421    *       <jc>// Process real field</jc>
422    *    }
423    * </p>
424    *
425    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this field is a synthetic field.
426    * @see Field#isSynthetic()
427    */
428   public boolean isSynthetic() { return inner.isSynthetic(); }
429
430   /**
431    * Identifies if the specified visibility matches this field.
432    *
433    * @param v The visibility to validate against.
434    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this visibility matches the modifier attribute of this field.
435    */
436   public boolean isVisible(Visibility v) {
437      return v.isVisible(inner);
438   }
439
440   /**
441    * Sets the field value on the specified object.
442    *
443    * <p>
444    * If the underlying field is static, then the specified <c>o</c> argument is ignored. It may be <jk>null</jk>.
445    *
446    * @param o The object containing the field.  Can be <jk>null</jk> for static fields.
447    * @param value The new field value.
448    * @throws BeanRuntimeException Field was not accessible or field does not belong to object.
449    */
450   public void set(Object o, Object value) throws BeanRuntimeException {
451      safe((Snippet)() -> {
452         inner.setAccessible(true);
453         inner.set(o, value);
454      }, e -> brex(e));
455   }
456
457   //-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
458   // Field-Specific Methods
459   //-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
460
461   /**
462    * Sets the field value on the specified object if the value is <jk>null</jk>.
463    *
464    * @param o The object containing the field.
465    * @param value The new field value.
466    * @throws BeanRuntimeException Field was not accessible or field does not belong to object.
467    */
468   public void setIfNull(Object o, Object value) {
469      Object v = get(o);
470      if (v == null)
471         set(o, value);
472   }
473
474   /**
475    * Returns a string describing this field, including its generic type.
476    *
477    * <p>
478    * Same as calling {@link Field#toGenericString()}.
479    *
480    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
481    * <p class='bjava'>
482    *    <jc>// Get generic string for: public static final List&lt;String&gt; VALUES</jc>
483    *    FieldInfo <jv>fi</jv> = ClassInfo.<jsm>of</jsm>(MyClass.<jk>class</jk>).getField(<js>"VALUES"</js>);
484    *    String <jv>str</jv> = <jv>fi</jv>.toGenericString();
485    *    <jc>// Returns: "public static final java.util.List&lt;java.lang.String&gt; com.example.MyClass.VALUES"</jc>
486    * </p>
487    *
488    * @return A string describing this field.
489    * @see Field#toGenericString()
490    */
491   public String toGenericString() {
492      return inner.toGenericString();
493   }
494
495   //-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
496   // Annotatable interface methods
497   //-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
498
499   /**
500    * Returns a detailed string representation of this field.
501    *
502    * <p>
503    * The returned string includes:
504    * <ul>
505    *    <li>Modifiers (public, private, protected, static, final, volatile, transient)
506    *    <li>Field type with generics (e.g., "List&lt;String&gt;")
507    *    <li>Fully qualified field name (declaring-class.field-name)
508    * </ul>
509    *
510    * <h5 class='section'>Examples:</h5>
511    * <p class='bjava'>
512    *    <jc>// Simple field</jc>
513    *    FieldInfo <jv>fi</jv> = ...;
514    *    <jv>fi</jv>.toString();
515    *    <jc>// Returns: "public java.lang.String org.example.MyClass.name"</jc>
516    *
517    *    <jc>// Static final field</jc>
518    *    <jc>// Returns: "public static final int org.example.MyClass.MAX_VALUE"</jc>
519    *
520    *    <jc>// Generic field</jc>
521    *    <jc>// Returns: "private java.util.List&lt;java.lang.String&gt; org.example.MyClass.items"</jc>
522    *
523    *    <jc>// Volatile field</jc>
524    *    <jc>// Returns: "volatile boolean org.example.MyClass.flag"</jc>
525    * </p>
526    *
527    * @return A detailed string representation including modifiers, type, and full name.
528    */
529   @Override
530   public String toString() {
531      return toString.get();
532   }
533
534   private String findToString() {
535      var sb = new StringBuilder(256);
536
537      // Modifiers
538      var mods = Modifier.toString(getModifiers());
539      if (nn(mods) && ! mods.isEmpty()) {
540         sb.append(mods).append(" ");
541      }
542
543      // Field type (use generic type to show generics)
544      var genericType = inner.getGenericType();
545      ClassInfo.of(genericType).appendNameFormatted(sb, FULL, true, '$', BRACKETS);
546
547      // Fully qualified field name
548      sb.append(" ").append(getNameFull());
549
550      return sb.toString();
551   }
552
553   private String findNameFull() {
554      var sb = new StringBuilder(128);
555      var dc = declaringClass;
556      var pi = dc.getPackage();
557      if (nn(pi))
558         sb.append(pi.getName()).append('.');
559      // HTT - false branch (pi == null) is hard to test: some classloaders return Package objects
560      // even for default package classes, though Java API spec says it should return null
561      dc.appendNameFormatted(sb, SHORT, true, '$', BRACKETS);
562      sb.append('.').append(getName());
563      return sb.toString();
564   }
565
566   /**
567    * Resolves field value from the bean store and sets it on the specified bean instance.
568    *
569    * <p>
570    * This method resolves the field value using the same logic as parameter resolution,
571    * supporting single beans, {@code Optional}, arrays, {@code List}, {@code Set}, and {@code Map}.
572    * The field is made accessible before setting its value.
573    *
574    * <h5 class='section'>Field Resolution:</h5>
575    * <ul class='spaced-list'>
576    *    <li><b>Single beans</b> - Resolved using {@link BeanStore#getBean(Class)} or {@link BeanStore#getBean(Class, String)}.
577    *       Throws {@link ExecutableException} if not found.
578    *    <li><b>Optional beans</b> - Wrapped in <c>Optional</c>, or <c>Optional.empty()</c> if not found.
579    *       Never throws an exception.
580    *    <li><b>Arrays</b> - All beans of the element type are collected into an array (may be empty).
581    *       Never throws an exception.
582    *    <li><b>Lists</b> - All beans of the element type are collected into a <c>List</c> (may be empty).
583    *       Never throws an exception.
584    *    <li><b>Sets</b> - All beans of the element type are collected into a <c>LinkedHashSet</c> (may be empty).
585    *       Never throws an exception.
586    *    <li><b>Maps</b> - All beans of the value type are collected into a <c>LinkedHashMap</c> keyed by bean name (may be empty).
587    *       Unnamed beans use an empty string as the key.  Never throws an exception.
588    * </ul>
589    *
590    * @param <T> The bean type.
591    * @param beanStore The bean store to resolve the field value from.
592    * @param bean The object instance containing the field.
593    * @return The same bean instance (for method chaining).
594    * @throws ExecutableException If a required field (non-Optional, non-collection) cannot be resolved from the bean store.
595    */
596   @SuppressWarnings({
597      "java:S3776" // Cognitive complexity acceptable for dependency injection
598   })
599   public <T> T inject(BeanStore beanStore, T bean) {
600      accessible();
601      var fieldType = getFieldType();
602
603      // @Value sites resolve from Settings/VarResolver, not the bean store.
604      var annos = getAnnotations();
605      var valueExpr = ValueResolver.findValueExpression(annos);
606      if (valueExpr != null) {
607         ValueResolver.checkInjectConflict(annos, this.toString());
608         var unwrapped = fieldType.unwrap(Optional.class).inner();
609         // Pass the declared (generic) field type so @Value Supplier<String> autodetects
610         // Field type IS the opt-in signal for re-evaluating reads (Supplier<String> autodetect).
611         // BeanStore is forwarded so caller-scoped PropertySource beans (e.g. per-RestContext
612         // @Rest(config=...) Configs) participate in expression resolution alongside Settings.
613         var resolved = ValueResolver.resolve(valueExpr, unwrapped, inner.getGenericType(), this.toString(), beanStore);
614         if (fieldType.is(Optional.class)) {
615            // VarResolver substitutes "" for a missing key with no default. Collapse both to
616            // Optional.empty() so @Value("${maybe}") Optional<T> behaves the same as Spring's.
617            set(bean, (resolved == null || (resolved instanceof String s && s.isEmpty()))
618               ? oe()
619               : o(resolved));
620         } else {
621            set(bean, resolved);
622         }
623         return bean;
624      }
625
626      // Find qualifier from @Named or @Qualifier annotation (same logic as ParameterInfo.getResolvedQualifier)
627      var beanQualifier = annos.stream()
628         .map(JsrSupport::qualifierValue)
629         .filter(Objects::nonNull)
630         .findFirst()
631         .orElse(null);
632
633      var ptUnwrapped = fieldType.unwrap(Optional.class);
634
635      // Handle collections and arrays
636      Object collectionValue = null;
637      if (ptUnwrapped.isInjectCollectionType()) {
638         // Extract element type from field type
639         Class<?> elementType = null;
640
641         if (ptUnwrapped.isArray()) {
642            elementType = ptUnwrapped.getComponentType().inner();
643         } else {
644            Type parameterizedType = fieldType.innerType();
645            var inner2 = o(ptUnwrapped.inner()).orElse(Object.class);
646
647            if (eq(inner2, List.class) || eq(inner2, Set.class)) {
648               if (parameterizedType instanceof ParameterizedType pt2) {
649                  var typeArgs = pt2.getActualTypeArguments();
650                  if (typeArgs.length > 0 && typeArgs[0] instanceof Class<?> elementClass) {
651                     elementType = elementClass;
652                  }
653               }
654            } else if (eq(inner2, Map.class) && parameterizedType instanceof ParameterizedType pt2) {
655               var typeArgs = pt2.getActualTypeArguments();
656               if (typeArgs.length >= 2 && typeArgs[0] == String.class && typeArgs[1] instanceof Class<?> valueClass) {
657                  elementType = valueClass;
658               }
659            }
660         }
661
662         collectionValue = ReflectionUtils.resolveCollectionValue(elementType, beanStore, ptUnwrapped);
663      }
664
665      Object value;
666      if (nn(collectionValue)) {
667         value = fieldType.is(Optional.class) ? o(collectionValue) : collectionValue;
668      } else {
669         // Handle single bean
670         var ptc = ptUnwrapped.inner();
671         var o2 = beanQualifier == null ? beanStore.getBean(ptc) : beanStore.getBean(ptc, beanQualifier);
672
673         if (fieldType.is(Optional.class)) {
674            value = o2;
675         } else if (o2.isPresent()) {
676            value = o2.get();
677         } else {
678            throw exex("Could not resolve value for field {0}", this);
679         }
680      }
681
682      set(bean, value);
683      return bean;
684   }
685}
